So the mechanic is:
1. Click on a gray dot along the diagonal path to move the purple square to that position
2. The purple square advances along the diagonal path toward the blue circle
3. Each move shrinks the bottom white bar by 2 pixels
The diagonal path goes from bottom-left to top-right (blue circle). The purple square needs to reach the blue circle.
Current purple square position: [50-52, 11-13] (centered around row 51, col 12)

Let me continue clicking on the gray dots along the diagonal path to move the purple square toward the blue circle. The path is:

So I need to click on each gray dot in sequence to move the purple square up the diagonal. Let me batch multiple clicks.

Next target: [49, 14]

After [19, 44], the blue circle starts at [11, 46] to [19, 52]. The gray dot at [17, 46] is inside the blue circle.
So the path leads to the blue circle. Let me batch the clicks to move the purple square all the way to the blue circle.
But wait - I need to verify that clicking on a gray dot moves the purple square there. Let me check: I clicked [51, 12] and the purple square moved to [50-52, 11-13], which is centered at [51, 12]. So yes, clicking a gray dot moves the purple square to that position.
Let me batch the remaining clicks to move the purple square all the way up the diagonal to the blue circle.
The remaining gray dots to click (in order from current position to blue circle):
[49, 14], [47, 16], [45, 18], [43, 20], [41, 22], [39, 24], [37, 26], [35, 28], [33, 30], [31, 32], [29, 34], [27, 36], [25, 38], [23, 40], [21, 42], [19, 44]
That's 16 more clicks. But I should be careful - maybe I need to check after each click if something changes unexpectedly. Let me batch a few at a time.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Path from current position to blue circle
# Current purple center: ~[51, 12]
# Need to click each gray dot in sequence toward the blue circle
path = [
    (49, 14), (47, 16), (45, 18), (43, 20), (41, 22),
    (39, 24), (37, 26), (35, 28), (33, 30), (31, 32),
    (29, 34), (27, 36), (25, 38), (23, 40), (21, 42), (19, 44)
]

actions = [{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c} for r, c in path]
result = action(actions)
print("Result:", result)

# Check final state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("\nPurple and key nodes:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['p', 'b']:
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
